metadata.notes:
agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: ready

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

**agent_supports: yes.** Aider 0.86.2 enforces a hard stop when a token/cost budget is hit mid-turn and resolves the turn cleanly. The cap surfaces through litellm's exception classification in `aider/exceptions.py`:

- `RateLimitError` (HTTP 429 / provider quota) is `retry: True` with the description "The API provider has rate limited you. Try again later or check your quotas."
- `BudgetExceededError` is also `retry: True` (litellm's own budget enforcement).
- The non-retryable insufficient-credits case (`APIError` with `"code":402`) returns `retry: False`, "Insufficient credits with the API provider."

The send loop in `coders/base_coder.py` (the `while True` around `self.send(...)`) doubles `retry_delay` (start 0.125s) on each retryable error; once `retry_delay > RETRY_TIMEOUT` (60s, `aider/models.py`) it sets `should_retry = False`, writes the error via `io.tool_warning(err_msg)` + `io.tool_error(description)`, and `break`s — ending the turn. The non-retryable cases break immediately. Either way the turn terminates and aider returns to its idle prompt; it does not silently re-enter `working`. There is no separate aider-native "subscription cost cap" feature — the budget is enforced provider-side (429) or by litellm (`BudgetExceededError`); aider's only knob is `RETRY_TIMEOUT` governing how long it waits before surfacing the cap.

**daemon_capability: full.** The aider adapter source is `FilesUnderCWD{.aider.chat.history.md}` with a markdown `RawParser` (`agent.go`). `io.tool_error`/`tool_warning` both route through `io._tool_message`, which calls `append_chat_history(..., blockquote=True)` — so the surfaced cap lands in the transcript as a `> ...Error...` blockquote line. irrlicht's parser matches this with `errorRE` (`^>\s*\S*(?:Error|Exception)(?:[: ]|$)`) and, while `turnOpen`, calls `flushErrorTurn` → emits `turn_done` with the error as `AssistantText`, flipping `LastEventType` so the state classifier transitions `working → ready` (`parser.go` ~127-205). This is the #262 path the matrix note cites.

The scenario's sole User-observable signal is "state reaches `ready`" — and the daemon reaches it today via the synthesized `turn_done`. The Primitive exercised says the budget cap "Maps to the adapter's standard turn-end path," which is exactly the mechanism the parser already exercises. The canonical spec signal is representable and handled, so this is `full`, not `bug`/`incapable`: a trace exists in the watched Source and the parser handles it. The two narrow gaps below are NON-spec-required, so per the authoring rule they live in `caveats`, not in a downgrade.

**driver_capability: ready.** No new driver step type is required. The error blockquote that aider's turn-end path writes can be elicited with the driver's existing `send` (a prompt) + `wait_turn` primitives by pointing aider at a tiny/bogus budget or an unreachable model (see the aider memory re: bogus-model errors producing `> ...Error...` blockquotes). The recipe needs none of the gapped primitives (restart/reset_session/sigkill/keys/etc.).

## Primitive → capabilities.json key

Primitive exercised = "Budget enforcement — the agent CLI enforces a token or time budget and emits a turn-end signal when the cap is hit. Maps to the adapter's standard turn-end path." There is no dedicated `budget`/`token_cap` key in `capabilities.json`; the closest cost-related key is `cost_tracking` (`false` — aider has no native cost-cap feature, the budget is provider/litellm-enforced). Because the primitive explicitly routes through the adapter's STANDARD turn-end path rather than a dedicated budget feature, the gating capability for recordability is `headless_mode` (`true`), which is the scenario's only `requires`. Confirmed: `headless_mode: true`, `cost_tracking: false`.

## Caveats are non-spec gaps, not a downgrade

The capped model call emits no `> Tokens: …` line, so `closeModelCall` never runs and `flushErrorTurn` carries nil Tokens/Contribution: the cap is observed as a generic error-terminated turn, not a distinct budget/quota event, and no per-turn cost is recorded. Neither is part of the spec's user-observable signal (which asserts only state, and `has_waiting_state: false`), so they are caveats — they would only matter to a future budget/quota-specific event kind.
caveats: record_blocked=infra (2026-05-28): axes are record-now but the cell is unrecordable in this environment — no aider auth (provider key) AND no deterministic aider quota mock (the claudecode mock-anthropic-429 stub does not transfer to litellm). Recordable once both auth and an aider-side quota mock / fixture-injection primitive exist. Not a driver_gap. (per scenarios.json by_adapter.aider)

feature-invisible-but-spec-compliant: the budget cap reaches `ready` via the generic LLM-error blockquote path (#262), so irrlicht cannot tell a quota/budget cap apart from any other turn-aborting provider error — the spec only requires reaching `ready`, which is observed.

metric-drift-downstream: the capped model call emits no `> Tokens:` line, so `flushErrorTurn` carries nil Tokens/Contribution — no per-turn token/cost is recorded for the budget-capped turn (capabilities.cost_tracking is false); only the error text is surfaced as AssistantText.

Aider retries a 429/RateLimitError with exponential backoff up to RETRY_TIMEOUT (60s) before surfacing it; during retries the session legitimately stays `working`, and only flips to `ready` once retries are exhausted.

Recording is blocked on environment infra (no aider provider auth + no deterministic aider-side quota mock — the claudecode `--bare`/ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL 429 stub does not transfer to litellm-routed aider), NOT on any daemon limit or driver step; expected.jsonl is committed as the documented target until that infra exists.

sources.note: Source is FilesUnderCWD{.aider.chat.history.md} with a markdown RawParser

parser.go ~127-205: errorRE matches `> ...Error...` blockquotes while turnOpen; flushErrorTurn emits turn_done (nil Tokens) → working→ready (the #262 path); closeModelCall only runs on a `> Tokens:` line
aider 0.86.2: RateLimitError retry=True 'check your quotas'; BudgetExceededError retry=True; APIError+402 'Insufficient credits' retry=False
send loop: exponential retry_delay, when > RETRY_TIMEOUT should_retry=False → tool_warning+tool_error then break (turn ends)
tool_error/tool_warning → _tool_message → append_chat_history(blockquote=True): the error lands in .aider.chat.history.md as a `> ` line
RETRY_TIMEOUT = 60 — the retry ceiling before a 429/quota is surfaced
headless_mode: true (scenario's only `requires`, gates recordability); cost_tracking: false — no native budget key, cap is provider/litellm-enforced

recipe.notes: scope_note: NOT RECORDABLE YET — assessed yes/partial (agent_supports:yes, observes:partial), so the cell is conceptually applicable, but it cannot be recorded in this environment because it is blocked on BOTH (a) auth and (b) a quota mock — NOT on a missing driver step type, so this is NOT a driver_gap. WHAT aider does: it stops cleanly on a 429/quota or budget cap — coders/base_coder.py's send loop doubles retry_delay until it exceeds RETRY_TIMEOUT (60s, models.py), then surfaces the cap (litellm RateLimitError 'check your quotas' / BudgetExceededError, both retry=True; or the non-retryable APIError+402 'Insufficient credits') via io.tool_warning+io.tool_error and breaks, ending the turn. The error lands in .aider.chat.history.md as a `> …Error…` blockquote (io._tool_message → append_chat_history(blockquote=True)), which irrlicht's aider parser matches with errorRE and turns into a synthetic turn_done via flushErrorTurn (the #262 path) → working→ready. observes:partial because (1) the capped turn is indistinguishable from any other turn-aborting LLM error — there is no quota/budget-specific event kind — and (2) the capped model call emits no `> Tokens:` line, so flushErrorTurn carries nil Tokens/Contribution (no per-turn cost recorded; capabilities.cost_tracking is false). WHY NOT RECORDABLE: (a) no aider auth in this environment — no provider key, headless aider hangs ~5min on provider OAuth, and the rig precheck delegates auth to the user (proven infra_fail). (b) NO deterministic aider quota mock exists: the claudecode mock-anthropic-429 path does NOT transfer — that recorder (tools/agent-onboarding/recording/record-token-quota-exhausted.sh) launches `claude --bare` with ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL pointed at an Anthropic-only stub, but aider's drivers (drive-aider.sh headless, drive-aider-interactive.sh) wire NO base-URL override and have no `--bare` equivalent, and aider routes every provider through litellm — so even WITH auth there is no deterministic way to elicit a 429/BudgetExceededError on demand. A recording becomes possible only once BOTH auth AND an aider-side quota mock (or a driver fixture-injection primitive) exist — a developer task. expected.jsonl is committed as the DOCUMENTED TARGET (ready→working→[`> …Error…` blockquote aborts the turn]→ready within the daemon sweep, single proc-<PID> session, final ready, never waiting). See replaydata/agents/aider/scenarios/token-quota-exhausted/assessment.json.
